100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 23, 1926, the British General Strike entered its third week, as workers continued to demand better wages and conditions, causing significant disruptions across the nation. In Germany, political tensions escalated as the government struggled to address economic instability and rising inflation. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate debated the future of Prohibition, amid growing public dissent against the law.
Updated: Saturday, May 23, 2026 at 07:50:03 AM CDT
